fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Vermont Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Vermont - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to vermont, speech-language pathologist compact vermont, vermont speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Office of Professional Regulation — Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Vermont Secretary of State, Office of Professional Regulation

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in Vermont.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: You must hold a master’s degree or doctorate in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Exam
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete a supervised professional experience (clinical fellowship) that meets the requirements outlined in the application.

telepractice_text: Vermont permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Vermont issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Vermont is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Vermont SLP license cost?
Is Vermont part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Vermont allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Vermont have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Vermont participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Vermont without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Vermont is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Vermont
hero.dek: Vermont Speech-Language Pathologist licenses require a master's degree, passing the Praxis exam, and completing a clinical fellowship.
